schemas.name: Auditor Appointment (ADT-1 Filing)
schemas.description: Auditor Appointment (ADT-1 Filing) by CA/CS firm. Expert filing and compliance. Virtual Auditor, since 2012.

step.text: File ADT-1 with RoC within 15 days of AGM
step.name: Step 4

mainEntity.name: When must the first auditor be appointed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Within 30 days of incorporation by the Board of Directors. Ratified by shareholders at the first AGM.

mainEntity.name: What is the auditor rotation requirement?

acceptedAnswer.text: For listed companies and certain classes of companies: individual auditor — maximum 5 consecutive years. Audit firm — maximum 10 consecutive years. Cooling off: 5 years before reappointment.

mainEntity.name: When must a statutory auditor be appointed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Within 30 days of incorporation (first auditor). Subsequently at every AGM. First auditor holds office until conclusion of first AGM. Subsequent auditors: 5-year term (individual) or 10-year term (firm).

mainEntity.name: Can a company change its auditor mid-term?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, but requires special resolution and approval from Central Government (NCLT). Casual vacancy (resignation): board fills within 30 days, AGM ratifies. Rotation mandatory for listed companies.

mainEntity.name: What is the penalty for not appointing an auditor?

acceptedAnswer.text: Company: fine ₹25,000/month. Officers in default: imprisonment up to 1 year or fine ₹10,000-₹1 lakh. NCLT can appoint auditor if company fails to do so.

mainEntity.name: What form is filed for auditor appointment?

acceptedAnswer.text: ADT-1 (Notice of Appointment) filed with RoC within 15 days of AGM. Includes written consent of auditor and eligibility certificate. DSC of director required.

mainEntity.name: Can an LLP appoint any CA as auditor?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. LLPs have no rotation requirement. Any CA in practice can be appointed. Audit needed only if turnover >₹40 lakhs or contribution >₹25 lakhs.
